task_completion.description: Open the Chinook database in LibreOffice Base
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Base application is open with 'chinook.odb' visible in the title bar or recent files list.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Tables' or 'Queries' section is selected, and a 'Create View...' window (either Design View or SQL View) is open.

task_completion.description: Define the view joining Customer and Invoice tables with required columns and aggregations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SQL editor or design grid shows a query joining 'Customer' and 'Invoice'. It must include 'CustomerId', a concatenation for 'FullName', 'Email', a COUNT function for 'InvoiceCount', a SUM function for 'TotalSpent', and a GROUP BY clause for the customer fields.

task_completion.description: Save the view with the correct name
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the name 'CustomerPurchaseSummary' or the view 'CustomerPurchaseSummary' listed in the main LibreOffice Base window under Views/Tables.

task_completion.description: Save the database file after creating the view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main LibreOffice Base 'Save' icon is clicked, or the File -> Save menu is used, ensuring the .odb file is updated.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Base GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Base interface rather than just typing SQL into a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The view was successfully created without lingering errors
integrity.visual_evidence: No unhandled SQL syntax error dialogs are left on the screen, and the view appears in the database's object list.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-made database file
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the step-by-step creation of the view (opening the editor, typing/building the query, and saving) rather than just downloading or moving a completed .odb file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a view named 'CustomerPurchaseSummary' in the LibreOffice Base database located at /home/ga/chinook.odb (HSQLDB 1.8 embedded). The view must join the 'Customer' and 'Invoice' tables. Required columns: 'CustomerId', 'FullName' (concatenating 'FirstName' and 'LastName', typically using || in HSQLDB), 'Email', 'InvoiceCount' (COUNT of invoices), and 'TotalSpent' (SUM of 'Total'). The query must include a GROUP BY clause for the customer fields. The final step requires saving the .odb file.
